paper_title: Distinct Directions and Distinct Distances in $\mathbb{R}^d$
summary: Conjecture 1.5 is explicitly stated as open in the source paper, including already for d=2. The paper itself proves only a weaker bound: Theorem 1.6 establishes that for all but a meagre set of d-norms, any d-dimensional set of n points determines at least (bd-o(1))n distinct distances with b ≥ 1/48, falling short of the conjectured (d-o(1))n. The precursor result of Alon, Bucć, and Sauermann (arXiv:2302.09058) gives the d=1 base case and the (1-o(1))n bound without the d-dimensional constraint. No post-publication resolution was found in the indexed literature through May 2026.
search_queries: distinct distances d-dimensional norms affine hyperplane meagre set 2025 2026
Alon Pinchasi "distinct directions" "distinct distances" Rd conjecture 2025 arxiv follow-up
notes: No follow-up found in web search. The conjecture is open per the authors themselves even for d=2; Theorem 1.6 in the same paper is the best known partial result, with constant factor b≥1/48 replacing the conjectured factor 1. Reference [1] (Alon, Bucć, Sauermann, arXiv:2302.09058) predates the source paper and is the prior work being strengthened, not a follow-up.
